title: requests — PHP attribute contracts
description: Two attributes. `#[AccessRequestable]` marks a class as a legitimate SUBJECT for access requests; boot-time discovery reads it into `AccessRequestableRegistry`. `#[AsAccessRequestListener]` marks a service that reacts to access-request lifecycle events; discovery wires it into the event bus at boot.

attributes.name: AccessRequestable
attributes.description: Marks an Eloquent model as requestable. Populates AccessRequestableRegistry at boot with the (resource_type, class, permissions) tuple. The `access.request_hint_on_denial` middleware consults the registry to decide whether to enrich a 403 body with the `request_url`. The `valid_requestable_resource` rule + `permission_belongs_to_resource_type` rule consult it at write time.

params.name: resourceType
params.description: Registry key. Must be lowercase snake_case (`invoice`, `athlete`, `trial_session`). Unique across the whole codebase — discovery fails on collision.

params.name: permissions
params.description: The permission names a caller can legitimately request on THIS model. Every string must be a valid permission on the `sanctum` guard for the caller's application, or the request is rejected with `PERMISSION_NOT_ON_RESOURCE_TYPE`. Example: ['invoices.view', 'invoices.update', 'invoices.export'].

params.name: displayNameSource
params.description: The model attribute or accessor used to render a human-readable subject label in the approver's inbox. Falls back to `id` when the attribute is missing / null.

params.name: defaultGrantTtlDays
params.description: Per-resource-type override for the grant TTL. Falls back to `config('access.requests.default_grant_ttl_days')` (default 7). Higher values are allowed but audited.

attributes.name: AsAccessRequestListener
attributes.description: Marks a service as an access-request lifecycle listener. Boot-time discovery walks classes carrying this attribute + registers them with the framework event bus. Preferred over declaring the listener in `$listen[]` on the ServiceProvider because it keeps the wiring local to the class.

params.name: events
params.description: Event class names the listener subscribes to. Every entry must extend `AccessRequestEvent` (see `events.json`). Example: [AccessRequestApproved::class, AccessRequestRejected::class].

params.name: queue
params.description: Optional Horizon queue name. When set, the listener is auto-wrapped with `ShouldQueue` semantics. Null = synchronous.

params.name: afterCommit
params.description: Delivers only after the outer DB transaction commits. Default true — prevents listeners from acting on rolled-back state changes.

discoveryNotes: AccessRequestableRegistry discovery runs during ServiceProvider::boot() at priority 34 (after workflow/approvals boots at 35 has not yet run — the registry is a passive lookup so the ordering is fine; the middleware that consults it fires at request time, well after every module has booted).
Discovery is cached in `bootstrap/cache/access-requests-registry.php` on first boot; refreshed by `config:cache` clear or `access-requests:cache-clear` command.
